title: digitalis cariensis subsp. trojana
intro: Digitalis cariensis subsp. trojana, also known as Trojana foxglove, is a subspecies of the Digitalis cariensis plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the foxglove family. The plant contains digitalis glycosides, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation, and are also known for their ability to strengthen the heart's contractions. The medicinal properties of Trojana foxglove make it beneficial for people with heart failure, as it can help to improve cardiac function and increase the heart's efficiency, thereby reducing symptoms of heart failure. Herbal preparations of Trojana foxglove can be found in the form of extracts or tinctures, which can be used to treat various heart-related conditions when prescribed by a healthcare professional.
